title: Crisp Syrian Date Salad
description: A fresh, crisp, nut‑free-friendly salad that pairs sweet dates with tangy pomegranate and a zesty lemon‑olive oil dressing, perfect for a light dinner or date night.
instructions: Pit the dates and dice them into small cubes.
Dice the cucumber, thinly slice the red onion, rinse and drain the pomegranate seeds, and chop the mint leaves.
In a large mixing bowl combine the diced dates, cucumber, onion, mint, and pomegranate seeds.
Whisk together the lemon juice, olive oil, sumac (if using), salt, and pepper to make a dressing.
Drizzle the dressing over the salad and toss gently to coat all ingredients evenly.
Refrigerate the salad for 10–15 minutes to allow flavors to meld and the vegetables to stay crisp.
Before serving, give the salad one final gentle toss, garnish with extra mint leaves and a light sprinkle of sumac if desired.
Serve chilled as a refreshing side or light main dish.
